title: Publish current preview changes to live (production)

requirements.text: Promote the currently deployed preview build (Version 36) to the live/production deployment so end users see all implemented changes (member pause-request status indicator, admin notifications, pause-request approvals/denials, and auto-expiry behavior).
acceptanceCriteria: The live/production URL serves the same UI and behavior as the current preview build (Version 36).
Both Admin and Member flows load successfully in production without requiring any new configuration steps from the user.
Backend canister upgrades successfully (including any required migration) and existing stored data remains intact after the production deployment.

requirements.text: Perform a production smoke-test pass immediately after publishing and address any release-blocking issues discovered during verification.
acceptanceCriteria: Admin smoke tests pass in production: Internet Identity login, Admin dashboard loads, pause-request notification section is visible, and pending pause requests can be approved/denied successfully.

Member smoke tests pass in production: membership ID login works, Member dashboard loads, and membership status/pause-request status is rendered correctly for active/pending/paused/denied/expired states.
Backend health-check (used by the frontend connectivity-aware loading) succeeds in production when the canister is reachable and surfaces the correct error UI when it is not reachable.

constraints: Backend must remain a single Motoko actor in backend/main.mo; do not introduce additional backend services.

Do not edit frontend immutable paths: frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.tsx, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, frontend/src/components/ui.
Use English for any user-facing text.
nonGoals: Adding new features beyond publishing the already-implemented Version 36 changes.
Major UI redesign unrelated to the release/publish request.
Changing authentication models beyond what is required to ensure the published build matches the preview.
